description: A free text field used to document whether the data in a repository is open access or controlled access. If access to the data are unrestricted, the acl field should be listed [“*]. Controlled access should be denoted as a comma delimited list of phs accession numbers, for example, [“phs000123] or [“phs000123,phs000124].
label: acl

description: Text specifying the kind of contrast used during the imaging acquisition process.
label: acquisitionContrastCode

description: The identifiable class of the study participant based upon their age.
label: adult_or_childhood_study

description: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events Version 4.0 Low Level Term Name
label: adverse_event

description: Age at the time of diagnosis expressed in number of days since birth.
label: age_at_diagnosis

description: Time from diagnosis to first event (or censoring), in days.
label: age_at_event_free_survival_status

description: The age (in days) of subject at a known exposure to a hazardous factor or substance.
label: age_at_exposure

description: The age in days of the subject at the time of the genetic analysis.
label: age_at_genetic_analysis

description: The age (in days) of the subject when the molecular test was performed.
label: age_at_laboratory_test

description: The age (in days) of the subject when the vitals measurement was taken.
label: age_at_last_known_survival_status

description: The age (in days) of subject when the response assessment was made.
label: age_at_response

description: Subject Age in Days at End of Treatment Course Day Count
label: age_at_treatment_end

description: Subject Age in Days at Start of Treatment Course Day Count
label: age_at_treatment_start

description: Numeric value used to describe the average number of days each week that a person consumes an alcoholic beverage.
label: alcohol_days_per_week

description: Numeric value used to describe the average number of alcoholic beverages a person consumes per day.
label: alcohol_drinks_per_day

description: Has the participant consumed at least 12 drinks of any kind of alcoholic beverage in their lifetime?
label: alcohol_history

description: Category to describe the patient's current level of alcohol use as self-reported by the patient.
label: alcohol_intensity

description: A decimal number capturing the ratio of variant alleles to wild-type alleles present in a sample.
label: allelic_ratio

description: The words by which the generally used name of the mutation is known.
label: alteration

description: The location within the body as captured in the topography label of the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, 3rd Edition (ICD-O-3).
label: anatomic_site

description: Text specifying the kind of contrast used during the imaging acquisition process.
label: arterialSpinLabelingContrastCode

description: The category related to contact with chemical, biological, or physical substances found in air, water, food, soil, or product that may have a harmful effect on a person's health.
label: asbestos_exposure

description: Text to identify the other name or alias of a person.
label: associated_id

description: A free text field used to document whether the data in a repository is open access or controlled access. Open access files should have authz = ['/open']. Controlled access files have an acl value format of ['/programs/phsNNN'] (e.g.  ['/programs/1234'].
label: authz

description: The average length for nucleic acid sequencing reads obtained after completion of the sequencing process.
label: avg_read_length

description: A high level grouping of the circumstance or condition of greatest rank or importance that results in the death of a living being.
label: cause_of_death

description: The type of read that contains the cDNA read:index1/index2/read1/read2.
label: cDNA_read

description: A description of the cDNA offset.
label: cDNA_read_offset

description: The size of the cDNA read. E.g. "98".
label: cDNA_read_size

description: The offset in sequence of the cell identifying barcode.E.g. "0"
label: cell_barcode_offset

description: The type of read that contains the cell barcode: index1/index2/read1/read2.
label: cell_barcode_read

description: The size of the cell identifying barcode. E.g. "16".
label: cell_barcode_size

description: A sequence of characters used to identify, name, or characterize a permanently established cell culture that will proliferate indefinitely given appropriate fresh medium and space.
label: cell_line_id

description: The method by which the file checksum was calculated.
label: checksum_algorithm

description: A small-sized block of data derived from a file for the purpose of detecting errors that may have been introduced during file transmission and/or storage and used to verify data integrity.
label: checksum_value

description: Threadlike structures made of protein and a single molecule of DNA in a human that serve to carry the genomic information from cell to cell.
label: chromosome

description: Physical map location of a Gene, expressed as either a cytological chromosomal band or by chromosome, start point and end point.
label: chromosome_location

description: The average number of cigarettes smoked per day.
label: cigarettes_per_day

description: A sequence of characters used to uniquely identify, name, or characterize a data file stored as an electronic record.
label: clinical_measure_file_id

description: One or more characters assigned by the Clinical Trial Sponsor to uniquely identify the investigational protocol or research activity.
label: clinical_trial_identifier

description: Organization or research site participating in a type of research study that tests how well new medical treatments or other interventions work in people.
label: clinical_trial_repository

description: The category related to contact with chemical, biological, or physical substances found in air, water, food, soil, or product that may have a harmful effect on a person's health.
label: coal_dust_exposure

description: The class or category of the secondary disease(s).
label: comorbidity

description: The class of clinical or laboratory procedure(s) used to determine the secondary disease diagnosis.

description: Text specifying the kind of channel of the quadrature detected data, or the combination derived from those channels, used to reconstruct the image.
label: complexImageComponentCode

label: consent_group_id

label: consent_group_name

description: A numeral or string of numerals used to identify the set of study participants who have signed the same consent agreement and that will be included in the dbGaP repository.
label: consent_group_number

description: Suffix applied to DCF indexing file record to note the appropriate participant's consent group.
label: consent_group_suffix

description: The number of times a particular locus (site, nucleotide, amplicon, region) was sequenced.
label: coverage

description: The crdc_id is a unique identifier that is generated by CRDC Data Submission Portal.
label: crdc_id

description: The curation status of the study.

description: The name of the custom assembly fasta file used during sequence alignment.

label: cytogenomic_platform

description: A text term based on categorization of the type of information contained in an electronic record.
label: data_category

description: Data location.
label: data_location

description: A stable unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to a study and any objects by the database of Genotypes and Phenotypes (dbGaP).

description: A unique sequence of characters used to identify, name, or characterize the indexing process that is different from all other UUIDs/GUIDs generated, making it possible to interoperate with data commons and share data within a data ecosystem.

description: The type of means, manner of procedure, or systematic course of action used in the removal of text elements that would make data individually identifiable in an electronic record.
label: deidentification_method

description: A written description of the materials and methods used to create the sequencing library.
label: design_description

description: The term which identifies normal and abnormal cells and tissues used to define the presence and nature of disease, as categorized by diagnosis_classification_system property.
label: diagnosis

description: The underlying scientific foundation used to determine the diagnosis.
label: diagnosis_basis

description: The term which identifies normal and abnormal cells and tissues used to define the presence and nature of disease, as categorized by diagnosis_classification_system property.
label: diagnosis_category

description: The standard or agency upon which the specific term used as the diagnosis value was obtained.
label: diagnosis_classification_system

description: Words that describe observations or explanations related to the diagnosis.
label: diagnosis_comment

description: A sequence of characters used to uniquely identify, name, or characterize the presence and nature of a disease or condition.
label: diagnosis_id

description: A term specifying the echo category of pulse sequences.
label: diffusionBValue

description: Text describing the kind of diffusion conditions for the frame, directional or isotropic, with respect to direction.
label: diffusionDirectionalityCode

description: The stage or period of an individual's treatment process during which relevant observations were recorded.
label: disease_phase

description: The text term to describe the method used to create single cells from tissue samples.
label: dissociation_method

description: A decimal number capturing the ratio of the DNA content or chromosome number in a tumor sample compared to that in a normal sample.
label: dna_index_numeric

description: A textual description of a storage location for digital data.
label: domain_category

description: A textual description of a storage location for digital data.
label: domain_description

description: The amount of exposure that a subject actually receives.
label: dose

description: The text that represents the planned dosage frequency for a agent.
label: dose_frequency

description: The text that describe the dosage route.
label: dose_route

description: The unit of measure for the dosage form related to agents used as part of a procedure.
label: dose_unit

description: The process by which an image is acquired or obtained.:An indication as to whether multiple echos of different phase steps are acquired using rephasing gradients instead of repeated 180-degree pulses. (BRIDG):The preferred Value Domain to indicate a question with permissible values of yes/no.
label: echoPlanarPulseSequenceIndicator

description: A term specifying the echo category of pulse sequences.
label: echoPulseSequenceCategoryCode

description: A measurement of the time in milliseconds between the application of the excitation pulse and the recording of the peak echo signal.
label: echo_time

description: The string of characters that represents the electronic mail address of a person.
label: email_address

description: The type of overabundance of transcripts seen in a sequencing library.
label: end_bias

description: The category related to contact with chemical, biological, or physical substances found in air, water, food, soil, or product that may have a harmful effect on a person's health.
label: environmental_tobacco_smoke_exposure

description: Identify the nature of the event that best characterizes the endpoint of the study.
label: event_free_survival_status

description: The text term used to describe a specific exon targeted or included in molecular analysis.
label: exon

description: The type of systematic investigation using controlled observations and measurements to test hypotheses or develop a plan of action based on the facts discovered.
label: experimental_strategy_and_data_subtype

description: A unique sequence of characters used to identify, name, or characterize the type of chemical, biological, or physical substances found in air, water, food, soil, or product that may have a harmful effect on a person's health.
label: exposure_id

description: The specification of a node (file or directory) in a hierarchical file system where an electronic file is located.
label: external_url

description: A sequence of characters used to uniquely identify, name, or characterize the domestic group of someone who takes part in an activity.
label: family_id

description: A sequence of characters used to uniquely identify, name, or characterize the state of connectedness between entities or objects referencing the state of connectedness between people linked through descent.

description: A enumerated value text field used to document whether the data in a repository is open access or controlled access.
label: file_access

description: A free text field that can be used to document the content and other details about an electronic file that may not be captured elsewhere.
label: file_description

description: The literal label for an electronic data file.
label: file_name

description: The measure (in bytes) of how much space a data file takes up on a storage medium
label: file_size

description: A defined organization or layout representing and structuring data in a computer file.
label: file_type

label: file_types_and_format

label: first_event

description: The name of the funding source organization or sponsor.

label: generic_file_id

label: gene_symbol

label: tobacco_smoking_onset_year

description: A record of an individual's background in regard to smoking tobacco. This would include such factors as start date, end date (if applicable), number of cigarette smoked, attempts to quit, and others.:The year in which an individual quit smoking.:The particular year an event has happened or will happen.
label: tobacco_smoking_quit_year

description: A category which describes an individual's history of smoking tobacco.
label: tobacco_smoking_status

description: Cancer stage as captured by the Toronto Childhood Cancer Stage Guidelines.
label: toronto_childhood_cancer_staging

description: Numeric value used to describe the number of cells used for molecular testing.
label: total_number_of_input_cells

description: Alphanumeric value used to describe the transcript of a specific genetic variant.
label: transcript

description: The name of the individual therapeutic agent(s) used as part of a treatment regimen.
label: treatment_agent

description: A unique sequence of characters used to identify, name, or characterize the kind of treatment administered.
label: treatment_id

description: In medicine, an improvement related to treatment. One or more characters used to identify, name, or characterize the nature, properties, or contents of a thing.
label: treatment_response_id

description: Text term that describes the kind of treatment administered.
label: treatment_type

description: The classification of a tumor based primarily on histopathological characteristics.
label: tumor_classification

description: Tumor confirmed not to be of mouse/EBV origin
label: tumor_confirmed_not_to_be_mus_or_ebv_origin

description: A text term to express the degree of abnormality of cancer cells as a measure of differentiation and aggressiveness.
label: tumor_grade

description: A text term for the origin of tumor grade permissible values.
label: tumor_grade_source

description: Extent of the distant metastasis for the cancer based on evidence obtained from clinical assessment parameters determined prior to treatment.
label: tumor_stage_clinical_m

description: Extent of the regional lymph node involvement for the cancer based on evidence obtained from clinical assessment parameters determined prior to treatment.
label: tumor_stage_clinical_n

description: Extent of the primary cancer based on evidence obtained from clinical assessment parameters determined prior to treatment.
label: tumor_stage_clinical_t

description: Code of pathological T (primary tumor) to define the size or contiguous extension of the primary tumor (T), using staging criteria from the American Joint Committee on Cancer (AJCC).
label: tumor_stage_source

description: The type of environmental, occupational or consumer-based exposure to airborne gases and particulates produced when materials undergo combustion or thermal decomposition.
label: type_of_smoke_exposure

description: The response that indicates which smoking products a person has ever used regularly.
label: type_of_tobacco_used

description: The offset in sequence of the UMI identifying barcode. E.g. "16".
label: UMI_barcode_offset

description: Unique molecular identifier; added at cell isolation or library construction step. Determines which transcript molecule the read originated from.
label: UMI_barcode_read

description: The size of the UMI identifying barcode. E.g. "10".
label: UMI_barcode_size

description: A decimal number capturing the relative rate of occurrence of a specific variant allele within a population.
label: vaf_numeric

description: Any technique used to validate PDX against their original patient tumour, including fingerprinting, histology, immunohistochemistry.
label: validation_technique

description: The year in which a subject was diagnosed.
label: year_of_diagnosis

description: Number that represents the years spent smoking cigarettes.
label: years_smoked
